thread.title: Sheinbaum Stands Out With G20 Proposal

title: Sheinbaum Stands Out With G20 Proposal
text:
Sheinbaum Stands Out With G20 Proposal
[Claudia Sheinbaum](https://x.com/Claudiashein/status/1858542535133798686/photo/1)
Claudia Sheinbaum met with several nation leaders, including the United States, to enhance cooperation. In addition, Mexico proposed a global reforestation initiative at the G20 Summit, urging nations to allocate 1% of military spending to combat climate change.
Ready for more? Here is your weekly roundup!
[Claudia Sheinbaum Strengthens Global Ties at G20 Summit](https://mexicobusiness.news/policyandeconomy/news/claudia-sheinbaum-strengthens-global-ties-g20-summit?tag=policyandeconomy)
President Claudia Sheinbaum held her inaugural meetings with several heads of state, most notably US President Joe Biden and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, during the G20 summit in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Discussions focused on key issues such as migration, security, and economic relations.
[Mexico Proposes Global Reforestation Fund at G20 Summit](https://mexicobusiness.news/policyandeconomy/news/mexico-proposes-global-reforestation-fund-g20-summit?tag=policyandeconomy)
Sheinbaum called for a global reforestation initiative, proposing that nations allocate 1% of their military spending to fund the largest reforestation program in history. Sheinbaum's proposal aims to mitigate climate change, reduce poverty, and promote global peace.
[COP29 Advances Carbon Market Regulations to Drive Climate Action](https://mexicobusiness.news/policyandeconomy/news/cop29-advances-carbon-market-regulations-drive-climate-action?tag=policyandeconomy)
At the COP29 climate summit in Baku, Azerbaijan, delegates reached a significant agreement on carbon markets, a financial mechanism allowing countries or companies to offset CO2 emissions by funding projects in other nations. This decision establishes common rules for carbon markets under Art. 6 of the Paris Agreement, a crucial provision that remained unresolved.
[Accusations of China Using Mexico Are Unfounded: Marcelo Ebrard](https://mexicobusiness.news/trade-and-investment/news/accusations-china-using-mexico-are-unfounded-marcelo-ebrard?tag=policyandeconomy)
Minister of Economy Marcelo Ebrard addressed recent allegations regarding China’s use of Mexico as a conduit for its exports, describing them as unfounded. He emphasized that Mexico has captured only 0.4% of Chinese Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in North America. “On what basis are these claims being made? We must avoid division and misinformation,” Ebrard stated.
[Government Cuts Funding for MSMEs](https://mexicobusiness.news/policyandeconomy/news/government-cuts-funding-msmes?tag=policyandeconomy)
The Ministry of Economy has announced a reduction in the 2025 budget allocation for MSMEs. Despite this reduction, the Ministry will continue fostering growth of small businesses by strengthening their integration into value chains, leveraging nearshoring opportunities, expanding access to global markets, and simplifying administrative processes.
[MORENA Pushes for Single Anti-Monopoly Agency](https://mexicobusiness.news/infrastructure/news/morena-pushes-single-anti-monopoly-agency?tag=policyandeconomy)
MORENA is pushing for a single anti-monopoly agency to consolidate the regulatory responsibilities of the Federal Telecommunications Institute (IFT) and the Federal Economic Competition Commission (COFECE). This proposed consolidation aims to create a stronger entity focused on addressing the country’s significant market concentration issues, benefiting consumers and SMEs.
[Claudia Sheinbaum](https://x.com/Claudiashein/status/1858542535133798686/photo/1)
